original: Possible date for establishment of the Iroquois (Haudenosaunee) League [disputed date - other research places date between 1450 and 1660]
Bishop Willebrand of Utrecht grants Swells state justice
German king Heinrich VII makes his son Johan king of Bohemia
Hurricane destroys ships off coast of Dominica
British kill 17 Stockbridge Indians in Bronx during Revolution
HMS Beagle anchors in Postage Praia, Cape Verde Islands
American blacksmith Micah Rugg patents a nuts & bolts machine
US Naval Observatory authorized by an act of Congress
Liberty Party nominates James Birney as presidential candidate
California pioneers organized at Montgomery & Clay Streets
Atlanta Campaign: Battle of Jonesboro, Georgia, 1900 casualties
Ottoman sultan Murat V is deposed and succeeded by his brother Abd-ul-Hamid II
1st US men's single tennis championships held at Newport, Rhode Island
1st major earthquake recorded in eastern US, at Charleston, South Carolina, 110 die
Phillies outfielder Billy Hamilton equals George Gore's MLB single game stolen bases record with 7 steals in 10-8 win vs Washington Senators in Philadelphia
The Industrial Conciliation and Arbitration Act passed by Richard Seddon’s Liberal government, making New Zealand the first country in the world to outlaw strikes in favour of compulsory arbitration
First professional football game: QB John Brallier is paid $10 and his team wins 12-0
Louis Napoleon Parker's play "Rosemary" premieres in NYC
British troops over run Johannesburg
Brooklyn pitcher Brickyard Kennedy walks 6 straight Philadelphia Phillies in 2nd inning of the Superbas' 9-4 loss at Washington Park; Kennedy still achieves his 4th 20-win season
Split skirt first worn by Mrs. Adolph Landeburg, a horse rider
New York Giants future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcher Joe McGinnity wins his 3rd doubleheader of month, beating the Philadelphia Phillies, 4-1 and 9-2 at the Polo Grounds
Mbunga rebellion takes German Fort Mahenge East Africa
Britain & Russia sign treaty with Afghanistan, Persia & Tibet
Britain, Russia & France form Triple Entente
A. J. Reach Co. patents cork-centered baseball
Swedish runner Thure Johansson runs world record marathon (2:40:34.2) in Stockholm
The "Sullivan Act" requiring New Yorkers to possess licences for firearms small enough to be concealed comes into effect
Massive protest rally on Sackville Street attacked by the Dublin Metropolitan Police; two strikers killed by the police
Soccer club PSV forms in Eindhoven, Netherlands
24.8 cm rainfall at Bloomingdale, Michigan (state record)
Ecuador becomes a signatory to the Buenos Aires copyright treaty
General von Kluck decides not to attack Paris
German troops reconquer Soldau/Neidenburg East-Prussia
Brazil becomes a signatory to the Buenos Aires copyright treaty
Chicago White Sox Jimmy Lavender no-hits NY Giants, 2-0
Oscar Asche's musical "Chu Chin Chow" premieres in London
Boston Red Sox, win earliest AL pennent ever (season ended Sept 2)
John Reed co-founds the short-lived Communist Labor Party of America after being expelled from the National Socialist Convention in Chicago, Illinois
Petlyura's Ukrainian Army kills 35 members of a Jewish defense group
Ukrainian (Petlyura) Army recaptures Kiev
Belgium starts paying old age pensions
Detroit radio station 8MK (now WWJ, 950AM) is 1st to broadcast a news program on the air
Italian troops occupy Corfu
League of Nations gives Belgium mandate of Ruanda-Urundi (was German)
1st NFL Chicago All-Star Game: Chicago Bears 0, All-Stars 0 (79,432)
1st national skeet championship in Indianapolis
Chicago White Sox Vern Kennedy no-hits Cleveland Indians, 5-0
Russian miner Aleksey Stachanov digs 102 tons of coal in under 6 hours, using a jackhammer
Det's rookie Rudy York sets record for HRs of 18 HRs in August
5th NFL Chicago All-Star Game: All-Stars 28, Washington 16 (74,250)
Japanese invasion army driven out of Mongolia
Nazi SS agents disguised as Polish soldiers attack a radio station in Gleiwitz, Germany and kill a local farmer; alleged assault used as justification to invade Poland
1st edition of illegal opposition newspaper Free Netherlands
German occupiers in Netherlands begin soap rationing
RAF Fighter Command loses 39 aircraft against Luftwaffe's 41
US National Guard assembles
Radio program "Great Gildersleeve," a spin-off of "Fibber McGee & Molly", debuts on NBC
Battle at Alam Halfa: German & Italians assault
U-boats sink and damage 131 allied ships this month (639,946 tons)
1st battle of Essex/new Yorktown: US assault on Marcus Island
Japanese occupiers intern Jewish Congregation of Sorabajo
Allied offensive at "Gothen-linie" Italy
French provisional government moves from Algiers to Paris
French troops liberate Bordeaux
Soviet & Romanian troops march into Bucharest
Foghorn Leghorn, a Warner Bros. cartoon character created by Robert McKimson and Warren Foster (Looney Tunes and Merrie Melodies series), debuts in "Walky Talky Hawky"
Hungarian communist party wins election
NY Giants set a season record for HRs by a club with 183 and end the season with 221
KRBC TV channel 9 in Abilene, TX (NBC) begins broadcasting
WKBG (now WLVI) TV channel 56 in Cambridge-Boston, MA (IND) begins
Cincinnati 1st baseman Ted Kluszewski hits 2 HRs in a 9-3 loss v Phillies at Connie Mack Stadium, Philadelphia; 1st Redleg to ever hit 40 MLB HRs, en route to season total 49
Hurricane Carol hits New England, 70 die. Costliest ever hurricane at the time and 1st storm name to be retired.
Indians beat Yanks 6-1 for record tying 26 wins in August (1931 A's)
WMTW TV channel 8 in Portland-Poland Spring, ME (ABC) begins
First sun-powered automobile is demonstrated in Chicago, Illinois
KTRE TV channel 9 begins broadcasting in Lufkin, Texas (ABC/NBC affiliate)
Agricultural Hall of Fame forms
US sweeps the medals in the shot put at the Rome Olympics; Bill Nieder wins gold with 19.68m, ahead of countrymen Parry O'Brien and Dallas Long
Amsterdam National Ballet forms
Trinidad & Tobago gain independence from Britain (National Day) with Eric Williams as first Prime Minister
Ground is broken for Anaheim Stadium, future home of MLB California Angels
The Aero Spacelines Super Guppy Aircraft makes its first flight.
US Congress establishes Department of Housing & Urban Development
Referee Leo Horn whistles his last soccer match (Ajax-Bulgaria)
12,000 die in 7.8 quake which destroys 60,000 buildings in NE Iran
68th US Golf Amateur Championship won by Bruce Fleisher
Roy Face ties W. Johnson's record of 802 pitching appearances with club
Verne Gagne beats Dick Beyers (Dr X) in Minnesota to become NWA champ
Lonnie McLucas, a Black Panther activist, convicted of conspiracy to commit murder
Molukkers occupy Indonesian ambassador's home in Wassenaar
WKMJ TV channel 68 in Louisville, Kentucky (PBS) begins broadcasting
Adrienne Beames runs female world record marathon (2:46:30)
An inquiry into allegations of brutality by the security forces against those interned without trial in Northern Ireland is announced
American super swimmerMark Spitzwraps up the Olympic butterfly double with a world record 54.27 in the 100m in Munich, having already won the 200m in world record time 2:00.70
Australian swimmer Gail Neall with a world record 5:02.97 wins the 400m individual medley gold medal at the Munich Olympics
Pirate Radio Veronica moves into Scheveningen, Neteherlands harbor
Mexican peso devalued
Aleksandr Fedotov sets an aircraft altitude record of 37,650 meters (123,523 feet)
Ian Smith, espousing racial segregation, wins Rhodesian general election with 80% of overwhelmingly white electorate's vote
Spyros Kyprianou appointed president of Cyprus
Constitution adopted by Sri Lanka
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Comet Howard-Koomur-Michels collides with Sun
Donald McHenry named to succeed Andrew Young as US UN ambassador
Poland's Solidarity trade union federations forms and is officially recognised by the Polish government
The Gdańsk Agreement is signed, allowing Polish citizens to bring democratic changes within the communist political structure
Dirk Wellham scores 103 on Test Cricket debut, v England at Lord's
MLB Kansas City Royals fire manager Jim Frey, replace him with Dick Howser
USSR performs underground nuclear test
Edwin Moses of USA sets 400m hurdle record (47.02) in Koblenz
Pinklon Thomas beats Tim Witherspoon in 12 for heavyweight boxing title
"Prakas" sets trotting mile record of 1:53.4 at Du Quoin, Illinois
Night Stalker suspect that terrorized California captured in East Los Angeles
Aeromexico DC-9 & small plane collide in LA, killing 82 (15 on the ground)
Russian cargo ship crashes into cruise ship Admiral Nakhimov; 398 die
South Africa's longest mine strike in history ends

The Great Potato Incident: Minor league Williamsport Bills catcher Dave Bresnahan attempts to lure an opposing baserunner off third base using a potato carved to look like a baseball. Umpire calls runner safe, and the catcher is fined $50 and released by the team
5-day power blackout of downtown Seattle begins
Arbitrator George Nicolau rules owners conspired against free agents
Bomb attack on office of South Africa Council of Churches
Arbitrator T Roberts orders owners to pay $105 million for collusion
Dennis Eckersley saves his 40th game of the season
East Germany and West Germany sign a treaty to join legal and political systems
Colorado Rockies bat out of order against Montreal Expos in 1st inning
Houston QB David Klingler sets NCAA record with 6 touchdown passes in the 2nd quarter as the Cougars clobbered Louisiana Tech 73-3
Richard J. Kerr, ends term as deputy director of CIA
Uzbekistan declares independence from the Soviet Union following the failed coup in Moscow
Dynamite explosion in a mine in the Philippines kills 500
Howard Stern Radio Show premieres in Cleveland, Ohio on WNCX 98.5 FM
HMS Mercury, the Royal Navy's communications training establishment, closes after 52 years in commission.
Minnesota Twins beat Cleveland Indians 5-4 in 22 innings
Venezuela president Carlos Perez flees
Last Russian soldiers leave Estonia & Latvia
The Provisional Irish Republican Army (Sinn Féin) declares a ceasefire in Northern Ireland
Last episode of Rolanda airs
North Korea reportedly launches its first satellite, Kwangmyŏngsŏng-1. While the North Korean government claims the launch is successful, no objects are tracked in orbit from the launch.
LAPA Flight 3142, a Boeing 737-200, crashes during takeoff from Jorge Newbery Airport in Buenos Aires, killing 65 people, including 2 on the ground
The first of a series of apartment bombings in Moscow, killing one person and wounding 40 others.
A stampede on Al-Aaimmah bridge in Baghdad kills 965 people
Apple loses its patent dispute with Samsung in Tokyo, Japan
US PresidentBarack Obamaarrives in Alaska on a 3 day tour highlighting climate change
Violent protests in Kiev after Ukraine parliament vote leave 1 national guard dead, 100 injured
Indications that Neanderthals are the first to make glue out of tar are strengthened by research published in "Scientific Reports"
International Organization for Migration states 18,500 Rohingya Muslims have fled from violence in Myanmar's Rakhine state after 110 deaths, making for Bangladesh
19-year-old Indonesian boy is rescued after 49 days adrift when his floating fish trap becomes detached
Gunman kills seven and injures 22 during high speed chase in Odessa, Texas
India removes citizenship status from 1.9 million people in state of Assam, leaving them off its new National Register of Citizens
Major track crash during Formula 2 race at Spa-Francorchamps, Belgium kills Anthoine Hubert and critically injures Juan Manuel Correa
Minnesota Twins smash 6 HRs in 10-7 loss to the Tigers in Detroit; break MLB record by hitting 268th home run of the season
US missile attack in al-Qaeda jihadist training camp in Idlib province, Syria, kills 40
US cases of COVID-19 pass 6 million with 183,300 deaths with California (699.000), Florida (619,000) recording the most (Johns Hopkins)
Mary Peltola is the first Alaskan Native to be elected to US Congress, defeating Sarah Palin in a special election
UN releases report accusing China of serious human rights abuses against Uyghurs in its western Xinjiang region, “may constitute international crimes, in particular crimes against humanity”
UN weather agency predicts a rare "triple dip" La Nina pattern to last till the end of 2022, the first this century, likely to extend droughts in Horn of Africa, South America
US life expectancy falls to its lowest level since 1996 (76.1 yrs vs. 79 yrs in 2019), with COVID-19 the main contributing factor according to the CDC
At least 76 die in a fire in a office building housing migrants and vulnerable people in Johannesburg, South Africa
New computer model study suggests humanity was almost wiped out 900,000 years ago with just 1,280 reproducing individuals remaining
Two leaders of right wing group the Proud Boys given lengthy sentences for sedition for their assault on the US Capitol - Joseph Biggs 17 years and Zachary Rehl 15 years
Bodies of six Israeli hostages are found in a tunnel near Rafah, Gaza, shot just before their discovery. Hamas blames Israeli failure to sign a ceasefire prompting large Israeli protests.
Major landslide in western Sudan destroys entire village of Tarasin in the Marrah Mountains, killing at least 1000 people
Henry VIbecomes King of England at the age of 8 months after the death of his father Henry V - youngest person to inherit the throne of England
John II'the perfect' is crowned King of Portugal at Sintra, after the death of his father
Jacobite Rising 1745:Bonnie Prince Charliereaches Blair Castle, Scotland
British troops underRobert Cliveoccupy Arcot in India
Ralph Waldo Emersongives his famous "The American Scholar" speech to Phi Beta Kappa Society at Harvard College, Cambridge, Massachusetts, declares American literary independence from Europe
Crocker-WoolworthNational Bank organized in San Francisco
The body of Jack the Ripper's first victim,Mary Ann Nichols, is found in Whitechapel in London's East End

Second International Electrical Congress adopts the joule as unit of energy (after James Prescott Joule), the watt as unit of power (afterJames Watt) and the quadrant as unit of electrical inductance (later renamed henry)
BritishGeneral Kitchener's army occupies Berber, North of Khartoum
Thomas Edisonpatents the Kinetoscope [kinetographic camera], a device that produces moving pictures
Theodore Rooseveltmakes a speech in Kansas advocating a 'square deal': property shall be 'the servant and not the master of the commonwealth'
Sun Yat-senand his supporters' 'rump' parliament establish a military government in China and elect Sun Yat-sen as Commander-in-Chief
Mussoliniorders the Greek Government to apologize for the deaths of an Italian general and his staff on the Greco-Albanian border
Finnish runnerPaavo Nurmiruns world record 10,000m (30:06.2)
AnthropologistMargaret Meadfirst arrives in Samoa
Bertolt Brecht& Kurt Weil's play with music "The Threepenny Opera" premieres at the Theater am Schiffbauerdamm, Berlin
Committee chaired byOwen D. Youngfinalizes the "Young Plan" to reduce German reparations from World War I to 112 billion Gold Marks ($US8 billion) paid over 59 years
FDRsigns an act prohibiting export of US arms to belligerents
The Liberal Party of Australia is founded byRobert Menzies
QueenWilhelmina of the Netherlandscelebrates her golden jubilee
DodgerGil Hodgeshits 4 HRs & a single in a game vs Braves
Federation of Malayagains independence from Great Britain
Sandy Koufaxbreaks Dizzy Dean's NL mark of 18 strikeouts in a game
Private Eye magazine reports thatJohn LennonandYoko Ono's upcoming album "Unfinished Music No. 1: Two Virgins" will feature a cover picture of them nude
BeatleJohn Lennonleaves England for the final time, moving to New York City
American 4 x 200m freestyle relay team of John Kinsella, Fred Tyler, Steve Genter &Mark Spitzswim a world record 7:35.78 to beat West Germany for the gold medal at the Munich Olympics
Soviet gymnastOlga Korbutwins balance beam & floor exercise gold medals at the Munich Olympics; follows gold in the teams all-round
BowlerEarl Anthonywins PBA National Championship - begins first of his two three-peats
Emily & William Harris plead guilty to 1974 kidnapping of American publishing heiressPatty Hearst
Ángel Cordero Jr.becomes 3rd jockey to ride horses earning over $100m
Curtis Strangesets golf's earning for year record ($697,385)
Michael Jackson's "Bad" video, directed by Martin Scorcese, premieres during TV special "Michael Jackson: The Magic Returns" on CBS
Baseball outfielders Ken Griffey andKen Griffey Jr.become the first father and son to play on the same team, the Seattle Mariners; the pair hit back-to-back singles in the first inning and both score
William H. Websterends his term as 14th director of CIA - only person to head both FBI and CIA
44th Emmy Awards: "Northern Exposure" (Best Drama & Best Drama Writing),Christopher Lloyd(Road To Avalonlea), and Dana Delany (China Beach) win
50th Venice Film Festival: "Short Cuts" directed byRobert Altmanand "Three Colors: Blue" directed by Krzysztof Kieslowski are jointly awarded the Golden Lion
A Pentium computer running Richard Lang's ChessGenius 2 program defeats world chess championGarry Kasparov
Thierry Henrymakes his professional debut for Monaco in a 2–0 loss against Nice
Diana, Princess of Wales, dies in a car crash in a road tunnel in Paris
Don Mattingly's #23 is retired by NY Yankees
Eddie Georgeruns for 216 yards on 35 carries and scores a touchdown as the Oilers, playing their first NFL game in Tennessee, beat Oakland 24-21 in overtime at the Liberty Bowl in Memphis
"Fly," the 5th studio album by theDixie Chicksis released (Grammy Award Best Country Album 2000, Billboard Album of the Year 2000)
Dino Ciccarelliretires from the NHL
Barbara Pierce Bush, twin daughter of PresidentGeorge W. Bush, gives a speech to the Republican Convention
The Walt Disney Company announces it will acquireMarvelEntertainment for $4.24 billion
President Obamaofficially re-designates Alaska’s Mt. McKinley as Denali, its native American name
Brazilian Senate votes to impeach PresidentDilma Rousseff, removing her from office
SingerAkonlays the first stone for Akon City, a futuristic solar-powered city to be built in Senegal, saying it will be a real-life version of Wakanda in "Black Panther"
36-year oldCristiano Ronaldois confirmed as a Manchester United player for a second time when his transfer from Juventus is ratified; Ronaldo left United in 2009 to join Real Madrid
A major outbreak of cholera occurs in Soho, London; physicianJohn Snowcalls it "the most terrible outbreak of cholera which ever occurred in this kingdom"
Stolen on August 22, 2004,Edvard Munch's famous painting "The Scream" is recovered from a raid by Norwegian police. The painting was said to be in a better-than-expected condition.
